frames.title: Cage Line
frames.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers count numbers instead of names. You are Nine-Mark — thrall, fighter, and cargo.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quiet Counsel
frames.body: Wind carries iron smell farther than voices. Tracks braid, unbraid, and lie with professional cheer. Continue is the only honest verb left.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps do. Memory of cages arrives uninvited, then leaves when named. Another frame turns like a dusty page.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences. Steel rests light; resolve does not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass. Hope and suspicion share the same waterskin. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: A creek argues with stones like a tired magistrate. Rations taste like postponement and pepper. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

frames.title: The Binder Arrives
frames.body: A lean man in travel-stained grey tips his hat to the overseer as if haggling over mule feed. Halbrecht Quill — warrant binder, chain-buyer, and something like a scholar of ugly laws. He pays coin for your papers without looking away from your eyes.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Dust lifts off the track in pale sheets. You check the freemark and find it still yours. The next mile does not apologize.

frames.title: Paper Freedom
frames.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board. Freedom on parchment, conditional on service: hunt the guilty Quill names, keep your steel sharp, and ask no soft questions. He waits for your word.

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

frames.body: Three chain-orcs peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and says, “Show them the paperwork is yours.”

frames.title: Quill's Lesson
frames.body: “Steel answers collar-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage. You practice until dusk makes the road one color.

frames.title: Campfire Question
frames.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

choices.label: Then Candlemire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Cade Mire still owns the debt.

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the yard is real.

frames.body: A farmstead burned for unpaid tribute; the killer rides with a brand that matches Cade’s outer seals. Quill’s warrant names him Vern of Lowhedge. Catch him alive if possible — dead if the world insists.

frames.title: Vern Cornered
frames.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse. He begs for Cade’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with women’s names. Lyra’s sits three lines down, sold as “song-thrall, unbroken.”

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

choices.label: Press for Candlemire routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

frames.body: You say Lyra aloud until the vowels stop shaking. Quill packs Vern’s routes — or what remains of them — and points toward a freeman town that stamps men as unpaid labor instead of numbered iron. Dawn smells like pine and unfinished debts.

frames.title: Freemark Bridge
frames.body: Freemark’s bridge exacts a toll in stories, not coins. You tell enough of Chain-Road to pass, and hide enough of Candlemire to sleep. Quill buys ink, ball, and powder like a man preparing a small war.

frames.title: Which Warrant?
frames.body: Three warrants hang like wet laundry: a goblin raid-chief, a barge cutter on the Brand-River feeder, and a knight who sold villagers under Cade’s quiet seal. Quill lets you choose the first debt to collect.

choices.label: Hunt the goblin chief
choices.approach: Clear hills so freefolk stop paying ‘protection.’

choices.label: Hunt the barge cutter
choices.approach: Follow water toward Candlemire’s quiet supply.

choices.label: Hunt the selling knight
choices.approach: Strike the soft armor of Cade’s respectables.

frames.body: Grin-Nail offers quiet passage maps if you break his warrant chain and let him flee north. Quill’s face says law. Your wrist freemark itches like a warning.

choices.label: Take the maps; let him run
choices.approach: Trade law for Candlemire intelligence.

choices.label: Challenge him openly
choices.approach: Single combat for maps and honor both.
